## Summary

This PR implements the Phase 0.5 LLM-first semantic seed path for evaluation.

- Adds canonical seed artifact generation from manuscript text.
- Wires Phase 1A preparation to consume those seeds.
- Preserves fail-closed behavior for malformed/unknown quality states.

## Scope

In scope:

- LLM-backed seed generation for canonical Phase 0.5 artifacts.
- Processor wiring for Phase 0.5 artifact production/consumption.
- Canonical artifact type/registry/persistence alignment.
- Targeted test harness updates for current query-chain behavior.

Out of scope:

- Changing canonical job statuses.
- Bypassing Review Gate / Phase 2 preconditions.
- Introducing non-canonical artifact identifiers.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0
Head-SHA: 7f8d8d25

## Risks & Anomalies

- Main risk: fixture drift in test stubs when processor query chains evolve.
- Main control: keep Supabase stubs chainable and contract-aligned.
- No production contract relaxation is introduced.

## Unauthorized Input Sources

- None added.
- Inputs remain manuscript text + governed templates/schemas.

## Internal Process Leakage

- No new leakage of internal chain-of-thought/operator internals.
- User-facing payloads remain public-safe.

## Input → Action → Output

- Input: manuscript + canonical doctrine/contracts.
- Action: generate Phase 0.5 semantic seeds, then pass through existing gates.
- Output: canonical provisional artifacts for verified downstream flow.

## Public-Safe Quality/Status Metrics

- Public status remains bounded to safe job/phase outputs.
- No hidden provider-internal diagnostics exposed to end users.

## Runtime/Pipeline Expansion

No-Pipeline-Impact: N/A — this is a governed evaluation-path change and must be measured, not assumed absent.

- No hidden runtime fan-out added.
- Existing gate order and fail-closed semantics are preserved.

## Evaluation Process Change Declaration

Process Change: yes

- [x] Sequential phase-gate doctrine preserved (parallelism only within safe sub-workloads).
- [x] Phase 0 remains first and is proven before downstream processing.
- [x] Phase 2 remains blocked on accepted_story_ledger_v1 (Review Gate authority).
- [x] Phase 3 remains blocked on pass12_handoff_v1 and is sole owner of Pass 3B synthesis.
- [x] Deterministic quality gates run after Pass 3B and before completion.
- [x] WAVE remains post-evaluation (after evaluation_result_v2) and non-fatal to base evaluation.

One-line doctrine: The pipeline is sequential at the phase/gate level and parallel only inside safe sub-workloads.

Process-Change Impact Summary (required when Process Change: yes):
- Adds LLM-first Phase 0.5 seed generation.
- Keeps downstream consumption verification-gated.
- Preserves canonical contracts and fail-closed behavior.

Pass Selection:
- [x] Pass 1
- [ ] Pass 2
- [ ] Pass 3

## Contract Integrity

- Canonical job statuses remain: `queued`, `running`, `complete`, `failed`.
- Canonical artifact naming stays enforced across persistence/registry/processor.
- Illegal state transitions remain explicit failures.

## Behavioral Quality

This PR is not reducing intelligence.

- It improves semantic drafting quality by using whole-text context first.
- It preserves deterministic verification and policy gates.
